topic.name: Wildlife Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and amendments aimed at the protection and management of wildlife species and their habitats.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer...
the manipulation occurs on the producer's land...
Document 2: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 to prohibit any person from importing...
Defines 'breeding' as facilitating the reproduction of prohibited wildlife species...

topic.name: sustainable urban development
topic.description: The documents focus on strategies and practices for promoting sustainability in urban environments.

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc2: discusses solar and wind energy innovations
analyzes the impact of sustainable practices on climate change

topic.name: Social Policy Reforms
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving health, education, and welfare systems in the United States.

Document 1: Repealing Ineffective and Incomplete Abstinence-Only Program Funding Act of 2011
eliminate the abstinence-only education program
Document 2: Amends part D (Child Support and Establishment of Paternity) of title IV of the Social Security Act
expand the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury
Document 3: Examination of Exposures to Environmental Hazards During Military Service
provide expert advice to DOD and VA on matters relating to exposures

topic.name: Tax Deductions and Credits
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code regarding tax deductions and credits for homeowners and businesses.

Document 1: tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest
Document 2: increase by 25% the allowable tax deduction for mortgage interest
waive the requirement to repay credit amounts under the first-time homebuyer tax credit
Document 3: extend the tax deduction for new business expenditures
increases the maximum deduction amount for start-up and organizational expenditures

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning temporary suspensions and modifications of duties on specific goods.

Document 1: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 3 Chloro 4 Methyl Aniline.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on noils of camel hair.
Document 3: modify the article description of certain men's footwear
extend temporarily the reduction of duty on such footwear.

topic.name: Military Personnel Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the security, structure, and healthcare of military personnel in the United States.

Document 1: Enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations
submit to Congress a plan
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Air Force to maintain the current leadership rank
Prohibits the Secretary of the Air Force from changing the aircraft or core functions
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer such member to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
jointly provide such members information on the nature and quality of outpatient care services

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: The documents focus on the management, conservation, and transfer of public lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Sets forth requirements concerning the maintenance of viable populations of existing native and desired non-native species within each planning area
Requires the Secretaries to coordinate the management of species populations in planning areas of the System and the BLM
Document 2: Permits Clark County, Nevada, to convey approximately 51 acres of land in Clark County to the Nevada Division of State Lands
Provides for the reversion of such land to Clark County for management in accordance with the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act of 1998
Document 3: Authorizes the Utah National Parks Council of the Boy Scouts of America to convey to Brian Head Resort certain public land in Utah
for the purpose of operating a camp in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land to the Boy Scouts

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Services
topic.description: Legislation related to the enhancement and provision of benefits and services for veterans, including educational assistance, disability compensation, and healthcare services.

Document 1: Increases the amount of educational assistance payments made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
pursuing an apprenticeship or on-job training
Document 2: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation
for qualified retirees with service-connected disabilities rated as total
Document 3: Makes permanent the authority of the Department of Veterans Affairs to furnish hospital care
to a veteran who participated in a test conducted by the Department of Defense

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy Changes
topic.description: Legislative amendments focused on restructuring family-sponsored immigration and adjusting visa categories in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to replace existing family-sponsored immigrant categories
Establishes a nonimmigrant visa category for an alien who is a parent of a U.S. citizen at least 21 years old.
Document 2: Eliminate the diversity immigrant program
Provide an equal number of annual immigrant visas (55,000) to certain employment-based aliens who obtain an advanced degree in the United States.
Document 3: Adjusts to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national
Requires adjustment applications to be filed not later than one year after the date of enactment of this Act.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the regulation of controlled substances and the approval of drugs for medical use.

Document 1: amends the Controlled Substances Act to add certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I
has a high potential for abuse; has no currently accepted medical value
Document 2: amends the Controlled Substances Act to modify the application of a provision that limits advertisements
prohibition does not apply with respect to an advertisement for a marijuana-related activity that complies with state law
Document 3: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to permit the Food and Drug Administration to allow the sponsor of a new drug or biological product
must use nucleic acids (e.g., DNA) or similar chemicals, or must affect a disease-causing product of a mutated gene

topic.name: Airline Passenger Rights and Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting the rights and safety of airline passengers during delays and security screenings.

Document 1: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2009
Requires each air carrier and airport operator to submit for approval by the Secretary of Transportation a proposed contingency plan
Document 2: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2011
Requires an air carrier to provide passengers on a departure- or arrival-delayed grounded aircraft with adequate food, water, restrooms, ventilation, and medical services
Document 3: Fair, Accurate, Secure, and Timely Redress Act of 2009
Establish a timely and fair process for individuals who believe they were delayed or prohibited from boarding a commercial aircraft because they were wrongly identified as a threat

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to labor rights, employment standards, and benefits for workers.

Document 1: amends the Black Lung Benefits Act to revise requirements for miners' claims for pneumoconiosis (black lung) benefits
Labor must report to Congress a strategy to reduce the backlog of black lung cases pending before the Office of Administrative Law
Document 2: Allows a pilot who has attained 60 years of age to serve as a passenger airline pilot until the age of 65 years old
Terminates the age-60 rule (mandatory retirement age) 30 days after enactment of this Act
Document 3: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted
Allows liability to accrue, and allows an aggrieved person to obtain relief, including recovery of back pay

topic.name: Government Property and Funding
topic.description: The documents discuss government measures related to the conveyance of property and the allocation of funds for public facilities and restoration projects.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the General Services Administration (GSA) to convey property in Anchorage, Alaska.
Makes the net proceeds from such conveyance available to the Archivist to fund repairs and restorations.
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Corporal Juan Mariel Alcantara Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Requires funds generated by fees collected for admission to recreation facilities to be used for repairs.
Used to cover the cost of repairing any federally owned recreation structure at Lake Texoma.

topic.name: Workforce and Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing workforce development, compliance relief for small businesses, and infrastructure improvements to support local economies.

1: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to authorize competitive grants to promote local economic growth
eliminate gaps between the workforce skills available and the workforce skills needed
2: Directs the SEC to modify regulations under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act for small businesses
exempt a non-accelerated filer from certain audit requirements

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation and policies governing the operations, regulations, and responsibilities of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: policy of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads
changes in pay policies and schedules and fringe benefit programs
Document 2: removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue semipostal stamps
USPS issues semipostals using its own discretion
Document 3: amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to use the mail
publish personally identifiable information of another person

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating contributions and expenditures in political campaigns and elections.

Document 1: prohibit corporations from making any expenditure for electioneering communication
employ or retain a registered lobbyist
Document 2: apply the prohibition against the conversion to personal use of contributions
allow the use of contributions accepted by political committees for authorized expenditures

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at protecting children through parental involvement in medical decisions and enhancing child care safety standards.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires parental involvement in the minor's abortion decision
Document 2: minimum health and safety training appropriate to a child care provider
training on cardiopulmonary resuscitation, first aid, safe sleep practices

topic.name: Public Land Management
topic.description: Legislation concerning the management, conveyance, and protection of public lands in the United States.

Document 1: convey to the Powell Recreation District in Wyoming
used for a shooting range, or for any other public purpose
Document 2: Withdraws the approximately 1,068,908 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District
from entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Policies
topic.description: A collection of legislative measures aimed at addressing various societal issues including gun rights, land preservation, and education reform.

Document 1: Respecting States Rights and Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2008
reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states
Document 2: Rim of the Valley Corridor Preservation Act
adjusts the boundary of the Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area
Document 3: Early Intervention for Graduation Success Authorization Act of 2011
amends the School Dropout Prevention program under part H of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965

topic.name: Environmental and Energy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to energy production, environmental health, and disaster management.

Document 1: impose specified sanctions against a person that conducts any transaction with... involved in the production, extraction, or transportation of petroleum
provides financing or other services with respect to such activities
Document 2: Allows the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to designate an area as an air and health quality empowerment zone
strategic plan designed to address air quality challenges, achieve attainment of air quality standards, and improve the health of the population in the area
Document 3: bars from taking effect for a 12-month period any increases in mandatory chargeable risk premium rates for flood insurance coverage
increases in chargeable risk premium rates shall be made on a yearly basis

topic.name: Legislation on Social Justice and Health
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at addressing social justice issues, particularly in health care, drug abuse, and employment discrimination.

Document 1: Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act of 2014 - Directs the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convene a Pain Management Best Practices Interagency Task Force.
Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to address drug abuse, including for educational efforts, communitywide strategies that address local drug crises.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or a prison term of up to two years on any person who travels in interstate or foreign commerce to evade compliance with a court-ordered property distribution in a divorce proceeding.
Document 3: Amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations, proceedings, or litigation under such Act was a motivating factor for any unlawful employment practice.

topic.name: Government Funding and Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and acts aimed at managing and distributing government funds for public programs and investments.

Document 1: modify and update the recovery.gov website
catalogues all programs and activities under the Act
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to guarantee eligible investments
caps the maximum amount of investments by such a capital infusion fund at $50 billion
Document 3: requires specified funds to be transferred to the Secretary of Health and Human Services
ensure that funds be used only to provide treatments to individuals who were eligible to participate in the state program

topic.name: Federal Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at addressing specific national goals and improving systems related to energy production, law enforcement communication, and civil service employment.

Document 1: Declares a national goal to achieve 20% of total electrical energy production in the United States from geothermal resources by 2030.
Establishes a national geothermal initiative under which the federal government shall seek to achieve the national goal.
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to establish a national Blue Alert communications network within the Department of Justice.
Sets forth the duties of the national coordinator, including providing assistance to states and local governments that are using Blue Alert plans.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting ecosystems and preventing the spread of invasive species and environmental hazards.

Document 1: Requires the Chief of Engineers, the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS), the Director of the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to take actions to prevent the transfer of aquatic invasive species.

Directs the EPA Administrator to coordinate with the governor of Illinois, the city of Chicago, and the Metropolitan Water Reclamation District of Greater Chicago to carry out engineering and construction of flood mitigation and water quality measures.
